compiled_state.title: Ticket system boundary enforcement and documentation sweep
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

After Epics A1, A2, and B add the new ticket CLI subcommands and the FILE_IMPACT event type, four scripts still access .tickets-tracker/ directly and 290+ references across skills, workflows, agents, and CLAUDE.md still point to old script names or describe direct .tickets-tracker/ access patterns. Without enforcement, the boundary will erode as new scripts are written — a DSO plugin contributor who writes a new script has no guardrail today; they copy sprint-next-batch.sh patterns and inadvertently bypass the ticket API, creating audit and testability gaps. This epic gives that contributor a fast-fail pre-commit hook with an actionable error and a CLI replacement, so the correct path is also the easy path.

## Success Criteria

- Four scripts that currently access .tickets-tracker/ directly are refactored to use ticket CLI subcommands: sprint-next-batch.sh calls ticket next-batch (preserving full flag surface — flags, output format, and exit codes are identical so callers require no update); sprint-list-epics.sh calls ticket list-epics; purge-non-project-tickets.sh calls ticket purge-bridge; pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh calls ticket exists. All subprocess calls use the ticket dispatcher shim (.claude/scripts/dso ticket <subcommand>), consistent with check-shim-refs.sh enforcement. Each refactored script passes its existing test suite with no behavioral regression. Scripts that become thin wrappers retain their filename for shim compatibility and are added to the boundary hook allowlist with a comment citing this epic.
- A check-tickets-boundary.sh pre-commit hook is added to .pre-commit-config.yaml that exits non-zero when any staged non-ticket-system file contains: (a) a direct .tickets-tracker/ reference for ticket data access, or (b) a reference to an absorbed script by its original filename. The hook's closed allowlist contains exactly: ticket-system scripts (plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-*), .suggestions/ accessors (suggestion-record.sh, retro-gather.sh), .review-events/ accessors (emit-review-event.sh, review-stats.sh), exclusion-pattern scripts (capture-review-diff.sh, skip-review-check.sh, compute-diff-hash.sh), thin wrapper scripts from SC1, and legitimate structural documentation (files under plugins/dso/docs/ that describe the directory layout rather than access it — path-scoped exclusion). Adding to the allowlist requires a comment citing the tracking ticket.
- All document references in skills, workflows, agent definitions, and CLAUDE.md are updated: references to absorbed scripts use the new ticket <subcommand> syntax; references to direct .tickets-tracker/ access for ticket data are replaced with ticket CLI equivalents. No backward-compatibility shims. The document sweep ships in the same commit batch as the SC1 script refactors to prevent live session regressions in skills that invoke old script names directly. Verified by check-tickets-boundary.sh passing in CI with zero violations.
- test-check-tickets-boundary.sh validates the hook with at minimum: (1) a synthetic violator exits non-zero with an actionable error naming the disallowed reference and the CLI replacement; (2) a staged change to each allowlist member (compute-diff-hash.sh, emit-review-event.sh, suggestion-record.sh, retro-gather.sh, capture-review-diff.sh, skip-review-check.sh) passes with exit 0, confirming the allowlist is not over-broad.

## Dependencies

- Epic A1 (2c28-e5b9 — Core Query Layer): mandatory — next-batch, list-descendants, list-epics, exists must exist before callers can be migrated and the boundary hook can enforce
- Epic A2 (4931-f639 — Validation & Classification Layer): mandatory — validate, classify, etc. must exist before the doc sweep can reference them
- Epic B (86c8-2d40 — File Impact): partial — file impact document references benefit from the sweep, but can be documented as "see Epic B" if B is not yet complete

## Approach

Three parallelizable work streams: (1) four refactor stories (independent of each other), (2) boundary hook + test, (3) document sweep. Stream 3 is gated on stream 2 reaching a stable hook definition, not on stream 1 completing. The boundary hook follows the check-plugin-self-ref.sh pattern: scan staged file content via git diff --cached, match against disallowed patterns, check against the closed allowlist. Downstream consumer map: sprint/SKILL.md (12 sprint-next-batch.sh call sites), debug-everything/prompts/batch-conflict-matrix.md (2 references with shim-exempt annotations — update to ticket next-batch), preplanning/SKILL.md (sprint-list-epics.sh reference), brainstorm/SKILL.md (sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0 call). Atomic-merge orchestration: land hook first, then B-E refactors + F doc sweep on a shared integration branch + single squash-merge to prevent live-session regressions.

## Research Findings

- Existing boundary pattern: `check-plugin-self-ref.sh` establishes the grep-based pre-commit boundary hook pattern (scan staged file content via git diff --cached; match disallowed patterns; check closed allowlist) — `check-tickets-boundary.sh` copies this pattern with a different allowlist
- Closed allowlist format: explicit enumeration file (distinct name from `review-gate-allowlist.conf` to avoid collision); each allowlist entry comment cites a tracking ticket for future audit; allowlist additions follow existing `plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf` convention
- Shim enforcement: `check-shim-refs.sh` enforces `.claude/scripts/dso <script>` invocation; refactored scripts must use this shim pattern when calling `ticket <subcommand>`
- 3-layer enforcement model: (a) pre-commit hook (this epic), (b) existing tool-use review-gate PreToolUse, (c) runtime shim enforcement — documented together in hook definition so contributors understand the whole picture
- Script count audit: 4 scripts access .tickets-tracker/ directly (confirmed by grep); 290+ doc references (confirmed via grep across skills/workflows/agents/CLAUDE.md)

## Scenario Analysis

- Doc sweep ships separately from refactors → live session invokes old script name → skill fails (high, deployment): atomic-merge orchestration requirement — all 3 streams ship in same commit batch via shared integration branch + single squash-merge; documented in SC3
- Allowlist too narrow rejects legitimate new ticket-system script (medium, friction): allowlist member addition requires ticket citation in the file's entry comment; documented escape valve; pattern matches existing plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf convention
- Allowlist too broad permits future .tickets-tracker/ access drift (medium, design): path-scoped exclusions (e.g., plugins/dso/docs/ files that describe the layout rather than access it) + exact script-name matches prevent glob-expansion drift
- Refactored thin wrapper drops a flag (high, correctness): each refactor story's done-def requires existing test suite to pass without modification + preserved flag enumeration documented in SC1
- Pre-commit hook takes too long on large diffs (medium, performance): grep-based scan on staged files only (not full repo); performance budget tracked against existing check-plugin-self-ref.sh baseline
- TICKET_TRACKER_OVERRIDE / CONF_OVERRIDE env vars break under thin wrapper (high, testing): `ticket exists` subcommand must honor these env vars; dceb-2566 story done-def explicitly tests env-var pass-through to injected tracker
- Purge-bridge introduces network dependency via thin wrapper (medium, regression): purge-bridge subcommand is LOCAL-ONLY by design; story d2c4-5515 done-def explicitly forbids network deps

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered — bright-line none (boundary enforcement is internal pattern extension of check-plugin-self-ref.sh; no external integration)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications + hook behavioral logic (doc sweep across skills/workflows/agents/CLAUDE.md; new check-tickets-boundary.sh hook; ~290 reference updates)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-commit boundary check + bounded ~290 doc references; not volume-sensitive

comments.body: Adversarial review: 10 findings, 7 accepted (3 rejected: allowlist-coordination dep unnecessary because thin-wrapper filenames are pre-determined by SC1; purge-bridge negative test is implementation-level; data-driven test for boundary is implementation-level). Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-8c20e0fd310e4beb/adversarial-review-e1b2-2653.json
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: NOTE (path update 2026-04-23): Brainstorm SKILL.md now uses `sprint-list-epics.sh --brainstorm` (composite flag added in this refactor) in place of two separate `--max-children=0` / `--min-children=1 --without-tag=brainstorm:complete` invocations. Any sweep referencing the old two-call form should be updated.
